body:
## Summary

- adds a root `.skills/paper-to-site` skill for building hosted literature-template sites from arXiv/LaTeX/PDF/HTML/review-style paper sources
- documents the target content shape used by the HCLLM paper example: `/` as the main paper note, `content/<paper-slug>/` as the section tree, and `content/papers/` as the reference-note area
- captures cleanup rules for LaTeX-style refs, backlink hygiene, title/frontmatter policy, graph tags, root-page layout, standalone repo workflow, and validation checks
- updates `.skills/README.md` to list the new documentation-only skill
- rebases onto current `main` and restores the required `AGENT_ENABLED` pause guard for the site-preview workflow jobs so the packaged workflow tests pass

Closes #4.

## Validation

- `npx prettier --check .github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml .skills/README.md .skills/paper-to-site/SKILL.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm --prefix .agent test`

comments.body:
The PR is documentation-only: it adds `.skills/literature-paper-import/SKILL.md` and adjusts `.skills/README.md`, closing issue #4. I’m reading that linked issue and the existing skill conventions before assigning scores.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #7.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted PR review/comment feedback that warrants a new or updated user/team rubric. PR #7 and linked issue #4 document a repository-specific `paper-to-site` workflow, but that belongs in the skill/docs rather than an abstract active rubric. Existing rubrics already cover the reusable parts, especially reading linked context first and keeping docs in sync.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

title: docs: add paper-to-site skill
